title: Child-safety company pitch fragment + a note for Gabby
html:
Child-safety company pitch fragment + a note for Gabby

Source: 2021-12-01 Apple Note. Tiny but loaded — a one-line pitch for an unnamed child-safety/family-photo product, plus a tender pep-talk to a friend Gabby. From sophomore year at OSU.

TL;DR

  • Pitch fragment: "our company set out to enhance child safety, and capture the precious moments missed. We have done that with our product" — sounds like an investor-deck opener for a family-camera/baby-monitor product.
  • Mentions a "sliding chair piece" + "close piece" + Camille (room 422b) + Nelson office hours — likely OSIG (Oregon State Investment Group) team meeting prep.
  • Embedded pep-talk: "I felt that despair and was like damn I need to cheer up gabby. it's just a blip in your journey right, you're dope so keep striving for excellence. we don't know each other very well, but you are cool in my book."
  • The "child safety + capture precious moments" pitch reappears years later as SimpleBaby/BabyTracker in the 2024 questions notebook.
  • Tied to OSIG / DADCO investment-group pitch culture at Oregon State.

Pattern observed

Earliest seed of a child/baby product idea that resurfaces three years later as SimpleBaby (the iOS app demo described in the 2024-07 notebook). The "we have done that with our product" framing is suspiciously confident for a sophomore — sounds like he was practicing investor-deck language. Same idea, different generations.

Open questions

  • Was this a real company pitch (group project for OSIG) or an idea Yahir was workshopping solo?
  • Who is gabby? Connection to the rest of the OSU crew?

Related

  • osig era — Oregon State Investment Group context
  • osu
  • 2024 07 04 questions — SimpleBaby reappears here as a class capstone demo
  • gabby

related.label: Numbers in investors pitch — emphasize the software aspect (early OSU class pitch)
